_doc: Embedding anchors per canonical story. Each anchor is a 1-2 sentence English description of one angle of the story; encoding these and taking the mean gives a story centroid against which articles are scored (cosine), with softmax-argmax across the 15 story set assigning each article to its strongest story (PR2 Phase B). Drafted by Opus during PR2 Phase A calibration; per-language anchors will be added in Phase A.2 if cross-lingual cosine drift exceeds 0.10. NOT yet wired into canonical_stories.json — that swap is Phase B, gated on Phase A's benchmark verdict.

hormuz_iran: The Strait of Hormuz is a chokepoint for global oil shipping between Iran and the Arabian Peninsula; tensions over a possible US-Iran deal raise concerns about tanker traffic and energy prices.
Iran has signalled willingness to negotiate a new nuclear and sanctions deal with the United States, with discussions covering the strait, sanctions relief, and regional security.
Tehran's military exercises near the strait, including episodes labeled Project Freedom and Epic Fury, have prompted responses from Washington and Gulf states.
A US-Iran one-page memo or framework reportedly under discussion would address the strait, oil export levels, and prisoner exchanges.
turner_cnn: Ted Turner, the founder of CNN, has died; his career building the first 24-hour cable news network reshaped broadcast journalism.
Obituaries for the CNN founder cover his media empire, philanthropy via the UN Foundation, and his ranching and environmental conservation work.
Coverage of Turner's death revisits his founding of TBS and CNN, his marriage to Jane Fonda, and his rivalry with rival media moguls.
Reactions from CNN staff and competitors mark the passing of a defining figure in 20th-century American television news.
lebanon_buffer: Israel maintains a military buffer zone in southern Lebanon and conducts strikes on Hezbollah positions south of the Litani River.
Hezbollah and the IDF exchange fire along the Lebanese-Israeli border; ceasefire violations and Israeli incursions continue despite the November 2024 truce.
Lebanese officials and UNIFIL have condemned Israeli operations in southern Lebanon as violations of sovereignty and the cessation-of-hostilities agreement.
Civilians in southern Lebanese villages remain displaced as Israeli forces hold buffer-zone positions and target what they describe as Hezbollah infrastructure.
hantavirus_cruise: A hantavirus outbreak aboard the cruise ship MV Hondius has sickened passengers and crew; the vessel has been quarantined while health authorities investigate.
Health officials are tracing the source of a virus outbreak on a cruise ship, with passengers placed under medical observation and disembarkation procedures coordinated.
The MV Hondius hantavirus cluster is the largest cruise-ship rodent-borne illness incident in recent years; CDC and port health officials are involved.
Cruise-industry analysts examine whether existing sanitation and pest-control protocols failed aboard the MV Hondius after passengers reported respiratory symptoms.
vietnam_china_visit: Vietnamese Communist Party leader To Lam is visiting Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping on trade, the South China Sea, and bilateral party-to-party relations.
To Lam's visit to China marks his first major trip to Beijing since assuming the general secretary role; the agenda spans economic cooperation and political alignment.
Hanoi-Beijing summit-level talks during the To Lam visit address rail connectivity, supply chains, and South China Sea maritime issues.
Vietnamese-Chinese bilateral statements following To Lam's Beijing trip emphasise comradely socialist relations and rebut Western criticism of regional behaviour.
iran_nuclear: Iran's nuclear program, including uranium enrichment at Fordow and Natanz, is under IAEA inspection and the subject of new diplomatic talks.
IAEA inspectors report on the level of uranium enrichment at Iranian facilities; the JCPOA framework remains a reference point for negotiations.
Iran's nuclear archive, centrifuge capacity, and breakout timelines feature in Western intelligence assessments and policy debate.
Iranian officials defend the country's right to civilian nuclear technology while Western powers raise concerns about weapons-grade enrichment.
ukraine_war: Russia and Ukraine continue heavy fighting along the eastern front; Russian drone and missile strikes hit Ukrainian energy infrastructure while Ukrainian forces target Russian positions in Kursk and Donbas.
President Zelensky has called for continued Western military aid and sanctions on Moscow; NATO partners debate the pace of weapon deliveries.
The Kremlin's military objectives in Ukraine, including pressure on Pokrovsk and the eastern Donetsk axis, are met by Ukrainian counter-strikes deep into Russian territory.
Drone warfare, civilian casualties, and infrastructure damage on both sides of the front line shape daily war coverage.
china_taiwan: Chinese military activity around the Taiwan Strait, including PLA Navy and Air Force incursions, raises tensions with Taipei and Washington.
Taiwan's president Lai Ching-te faces Beijing's pressure campaign over sovereignty claims; Taiwanese defense ministry reports daily PLA crossings of the median line.
US policy on the one-China framework, Taiwan arms sales, and unofficial diplomatic exchanges remains a flashpoint in US-China relations.
Chinese state media frames Taiwan as an internal affair while Taiwanese politicians warn of Chinese coercion and economic decoupling pressure.
climate_policy: International climate negotiations at the latest COP summit address emissions targets, climate finance, and a possible new fossil-fuel transition framework.
Governments debate net-zero pledges, carbon-market design, and Paris Agreement implementation; developing-country negotiators push for loss-and-damage funding.
Climate-policy disputes between major emitters cover carbon taxes, emissions trading, and adaptation finance for vulnerable states.
Annual climate-summit communiqués, draft texts on phase-down language, and disputes over fossil-fuel subsidies dominate the negotiations.
ai_regulation: The EU AI Act and parallel US legislative efforts shape how artificial intelligence is regulated, including provisions on transparency, foundation models, and high-risk use cases.
Governments and regulators debate AI safety policy, deepfake election laws, and oversight of large AI labs amid concerns about misuse and concentration.
OpenAI, Anthropic, and other AI companies face lawsuits, FTC investigations, and content-moderation policy fights over training data and model behaviour.
AI-governance proposals span national-security export controls, watermarking requirements for generated media, and mandatory model evaluations.
israel_palestine: Israeli military operations in Gaza and the West Bank continue alongside ceasefire negotiations and humanitarian-aid disputes; Hamas, the IDF, and the Palestinian Authority remain principal actors.
Civilian casualties, hostage-release talks, and the question of post-war Gaza governance dominate the coverage of the Israel-Palestine conflict.
Prime Minister Netanyahu's coalition debates the trajectory of military operations in Gaza and West Bank settlement policy.
International mediators including Qatar, Egypt, and the US continue ceasefire shuttle diplomacy between Israel and Hamas.
india_pakistan: Tensions between India and Pakistan along the Line of Control in Kashmir continue, with diplomatic exchanges between New Delhi and Islamabad and disputes over cross-border militancy.
Indian and Pakistani officials trade accusations over Kashmir, water-sharing disputes, and cross-border infiltration; bilateral summits remain rare.
Kashmir remains the central territorial dispute between India and Pakistan; both armies maintain heavy deployments along the LoC.
Diplomatic correspondence between Delhi and Islamabad addresses visa policies, trade restrictions, and trans-border religious-pilgrimage protocols.
us_election_cycle: The 2028 US presidential election cycle is taking shape, with potential Republican and Democratic primary candidates positioning ahead of Iowa and New Hampshire.
Speculation around 2028 candidates including Trump-aligned successors, Harris-aligned Democrats, Newsom, DeSantis, and Vance dominates US political reporting.
Primary debate calendars, fundraising filings, and PAC formations frame early-cycle 2028 election coverage.
Polling on a hypothetical 2028 race, voter-registration trends, and state-level redistricting shape early electoral coverage.
eu_expansion: European Union accession negotiations with Ukraine, Moldova, Albania, Montenegro, Serbia, Kosovo, Bosnia, North Macedonia, and Georgia continue with mixed progress at the level of the European Commission.
Brussels' enlargement strategy, candidate-country chapter openings, and Western Balkans summit diplomacy shape EU expansion coverage.
Accession criteria including rule-of-law reforms and judicial reforms in candidate states feature in European Commission assessments.
EU member-state debates on the speed of enlargement and the absorption capacity of the union frame the political timetable for accession.
africa_political_transitions: Africa's recent wave of military coups in Niger, Mali, Burkina Faso, Gabon, Guinea, and Sudan continues to reshape regional politics; ECOWAS sanctions and African Union mediation remain factors.
African Union and ECOWAS responses to coups in the Sahel, including sanctions, mediation, and suspended-membership decisions, frame regional diplomacy.
Elections, transitions, and protests in Kenya, Ethiopia, Senegal, Nigeria, and South Africa carry continent-wide political implications.
Junta governments in West African states negotiate withdrawal from ECOWAS and pivot foreign policy toward Russia and away from former French ties.
